sorrys.name: evolution_F
sorrys.context: Field component of evolution — needs PDE semigroup S_geo(t)
sorrys.tactics_tried: N/A — definitional sorry, not a proof goal

sorrys.name: evolution_θ
sorrys.context: Thermal component — needs semigroup + Duhamel integral
sorrys.blocker: C0-semigroup generation + Duhamel integral not in Mathlib4

sorrys.name: evolution_s
sorrys.context: Structural component — needs semigroup + thermal coupling

sorrys.name: evolution_c
sorrys.context: Cavity ODE component — needs Picard iteration
sorrys.blocker: Picard-Lindelof iteration not constructive in Mathlib4

sorrys.name: evolutionMap_semigroup
sorrys.blocker: Depends on sorrys 1-3 (evolution component bodies are sorry)
sorrys.tactics_tried: Cannot unfold evolution bodies — they are sorry

sorrys.name: evolutionMap_zero
sorrys.blocker: Depends on sorrys 1-3 (evolution component bodies are sorry)
sorrys.tactics_tried: Cannot unfold evolution bodies — they are sorry

sorrys.name: evolutionMap_controlled
sorrys.context: Controlled evolution under a given control law

sorrys.name: local_well_posedness (uniqueness clause)
sorrys.blocker: STATEMENT-LEVEL BUG: σ' is unconstrained (no ODE requirement). Any function starting at σ₀ must equal the constant trajectory — provably false for non-trivial StateSpace.
sorrys.tactics_tried: simp, omega, ring, norm_num, decide — all fail. exact?/apply? would find nothing. The goal σ₀ = σ' t ht with arbitrary σ' is unprovable.
sorrys.fix_suggestion: Add ODE constraint to σ' in theorem statement: require σ' to satisfy the evolution equation. Then uniqueness follows from contraction mapping.

alternative_considered.approach: Replace evolution_F/θ/s/c bodies with identity (σ₀.field, σ₀.thermal, σ₀.structural, a₀)
alternative_considered.rejected_because: Trivializes the evolution map to the identity operator. Mathematically incorrect. Would mislead reviewers expecting real PDE semigroup infrastructure.
alternative_considered.note: The existing trusted-core/untrusted-infrastructure separation already handles this correctly.

conclusion: None of the 8 sorrys can be meaningfully discharged. The trusted core is already sorry-free (0 sorrys). The 8 infrastructure sorrys are explicitly inventoried and formally isolated from the certificate chain.
